title: Root (solution)
short: A value of the variable that makes an expression zero or an equation true.
full:
A root of an expression is a value of the variable that makes it equal to zero; equivalently, a solution of the equation 'expression = 0'. For x² − 4, the roots are x = 2 and x = −2, because both make the expression vanish.

Graphically, the real roots are where the curve crosses the horizontal axis. A polynomial of degree n has at most n roots. Factoring exposes the roots directly: from (x − 2)(x + 2) you read off x = 2 and x = −2.

Roots are pure numbers.
